steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a logical-completion question. The blank must follow logically from the preceding clauses, especially the phrase "placing audiences at a distance."

steps.title: Trace the logic
steps.content: Brecht wanted an intellectual rather than emotional response. Churchill's play places audiences at a distance to interrogate patriarchy and colonialism. Distance from emotion plus interrogation of issues means audiences think critically without being swept up emotionally — i.e., they are dispassionate.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'focus on the characters' beliefs... as revealed by the characters' actions': this misreads the passage, which says the play works beyond the characters and events onstage, not by drilling into individual characters' beliefs. ✗
  • Choice B — 'reflect on social and political phenomena not directly related to patriarchy and colonialism': contradicts the passage, which explicitly says the play interrogates patriarchy and colonialism. ✗
  • Choice C — 'recognize... parallels between Germany during Brecht's time and England': overreach. The passage mentions Brecht only to credit his influence on technique; it never claims the play draws historical parallels between the two countries. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
